head: 912f915dd0d339f8430389fc73cb6b66cd18e6a1

theorem_to_claim_mapping.paper_claim: A ProductIndex active set can be transported to Typed.WidthIndex and back through flatten/unflatten membership equivalence.
theorem_to_claim_mapping.scope: arbitrary ProductIndex active lists plus restricted-parameter instance
theorem_to_claim_mapping.unsafe_wording: full semantic equivalence of all VF-H2 active sets

theorem_to_claim_mapping.paper_claim: Pointwise predicates over product indices and typed width indices are transportable through the ProductIndex/Typed.WidthIndex equivalence.
theorem_to_claim_mapping.scope: pointwise predicates and active-list restricted predicate transport
theorem_to_claim_mapping.unsafe_wording: all higher-order semantic properties are transported

theorem_to_claim_mapping.paper_claim: Fixed-set conditions are preserved under Product/Typed transport for arbitrary ProductIndex active lists.
theorem_to_claim_mapping.scope: fixed-set condition used by current ProductTypedState scaffold
theorem_to_claim_mapping.unsafe_wording: all fixed-point or equilibrium notions are verified

theorem_to_claim_mapping.paper_claim: The active-update operation commutes with Product-to-Typed transport for arbitrary ProductIndex active lists.
theorem_to_claim_mapping.scope: active-update operation setting active coordinates to top in the current scaffold
theorem_to_claim_mapping.unsafe_wording: all VF-H2 dynamics commute under representation transport

theorem_to_claim_mapping.paper_claim: A generic update-effect expression is transported when update commutation and score preservation hypotheses are available.
theorem_to_claim_mapping.scope: generic effect pattern and restricted ledger-effect instance
theorem_to_claim_mapping.unsafe_wording: all causal effects or biological effects are verified

theorem_to_claim_mapping.paper_claim: Conjunction-shaped bridge targets are transported when their fixed and condition components are transported componentwise.
theorem_to_claim_mapping.scope: generic conjunction-shaped bridge targets and restricted bridge target instance

theorem_to_claim_mapping.paper_claim: The v12 generalized transport ladder certificate collects the verified transport layers into a single Lean-checked certificate.
theorem_to_claim_mapping.scope: certificate collecting v12 generalized transport layers; clean build at v12.7.2
theorem_to_claim_mapping.unsafe_wording: complete formal verification of the full VF-H2 theory

recommended_title: A Lean-Checked Transport Layer for Representation Consistency in a Restricted VF-H2 Scaffold
recommended_short_title: Lean-Checked Transport for a Restricted VF-H2 Scaffold
contribution_statement: The contribution is a Lean-machine-checked representation-transport layer, not a full VF-H2 proof. It establishes that the current restricted scaffold preserves selected structures and operations when moving between product-structured and typed representations, and it records these results in a generalized transport certificate.

allowed: Lean-machine-checked transport layer for a restricted VF-H2 scaffold
generic effect-transport theorem under explicit hypotheses
generic bridge-target transport for conjunction-shaped bridge targets
clean generalized transport certificate at v12.7.2
forbidden: clean theorem-bearing status for v12.7.0 or v12.7.1

abstract_variants.technical_note: We present a Lean-machine-checked transport layer for a restricted VF-H2 computational scaffold. The formalization relates product-structured and typed representations through active-set membership, pointwise predicates, fixed-set conditions, active-update operations, generic update effects, and conjunction-shaped bridge targets. The main contribution is a generalized transport ladder certificate that collects these representation-consistency results in Lean. The result is a formal consistency scaffold rather than a proof of the full VF-H2 theory or an empirical/biological validation.
abstract_variants.formal_methods: This work develops a Lean-verified representation-transport framework for a restricted VF-H2 scaffold. We prove generalized transport theorems for active sets, pointwise predicates, fixed-set conditions, active updates, generic effect terms, and bridge-target components between product-structured and typed representations. A final certificate theorem records the verified ladder of transport results. The formal contribution is limited to representation consistency within the stated scaffold and does not claim unrestricted VF-H2 correctness or empirical validation.
abstract_variants.very_conservative: We formalize and verify a restricted representation-consistency layer for VF-H2 in Lean. The development proves that selected active-set, predicate, fixed-set, update, effect, and bridge-target constructions are preserved between product-structured and typed encodings. These results support a machine-checked scaffold for later extensions, while leaving unrestricted theory, empirical validation, and biological interpretation outside the present claim boundary.
